report.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.
report.rootCause: The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.
mitreMapping: T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
timeline: 2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)
2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected
2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking
2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials
2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18
agentRouting: Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.
Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.
Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.
Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.
Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.
Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.
Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.
Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.
Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.
Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.
Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.
Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment.
containmentActions: Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration
Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions
Reset MFA factors for the affected user
recommendations: Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations
Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data
Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints
Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload
analystDecisions: Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity
toolResultSummary: VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.
AbuseIPDB: IP

responsePayload.reason: Supervisor starts triage because jsmith@ent.local, WS-FIN-042, and evil-cdn-update.net appear in the same alert cluster.
Triage finds enough malicious signal to enrich IP, domain, URL, and hash evidence.
Threat intel suggests the identity may be the attacker pivot rather than only the victim.
Identity risk is high enough to inspect endpoint process ancestry and network sessions.
Endpoint artifacts need SIEM and cloud timeline correlation before containment.
Map-reduce logs expose a second-stage IOC, so Log Analysis routes back to Enrichment.
New enrichment updates the user/session blast-radius hypothesis.
Updated identity evidence requires a second endpoint check on the affected host.
Endpoint confirmation sends the graph back into log reduction for a final confidence score.
Second-pass timeline has enough confidence for actor/TTP matching.
Threat Intel returns the case to Supervisor for the final containment decision.
Supervisor chooses HITL containment after cyclic review raises confidence above threshold.

Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage"], "timeline": ["2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)", "2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected", "2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking", "2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials", "2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18"], "agentRouting": ["Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.", "Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.", "Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.", "Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.", "Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.", "Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.", "Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.", "Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.", "Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.", "Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.", "Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.", "Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment."], "containmentActions": ["Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration", "Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions", "Reset MFA factors for the affected user"], "recommendations": ["Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations", "Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data", "Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints", "Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload"], "analystDecisions": ["Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity"], "toolResultSummary": ["VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.", "AbuseIPDB: IP"]}

parsedResponsePayload.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.
parsedResponsePayload.rootCause: The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.
mitreMapping: T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
timeline: 2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)
2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected
2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking
2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials
2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18
agentRouting: Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.
Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.
Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.
Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.
Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.
Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.
Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.
Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.
Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.
Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.
Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.
Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment.
containmentActions: Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration
Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions
Reset MFA factors for the affected user
recommendations: Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations
Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data
Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints
Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload
analystDecisions: Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity
toolResultSummary: VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.
AbuseIPDB: IP

parsedOutput.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.

Reset MFA factors for the affected user

parsedResponsePayload.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.
parsedResponsePayload.rootCause: The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.
mitreMapping: T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
timeline: 2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)
2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected
2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking
2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials
2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18
agentRouting: Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.
Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.
Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.
Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.
Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.
Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.
Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.
Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.
Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.
Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.
Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.
Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment.
containmentActions: Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration
Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions
Reset MFA factors for the affected user
recommendations: Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations
Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data
Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints
Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload
analystDecisions: Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity
toolResultSummary: VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.
AbuseIPDB: IP

responsePayload.reason: Supervisor starts triage because jsmith@ent.local, WS-FIN-042, and evil-cdn-update.net appear in the same alert cluster.
Triage finds enough malicious signal to enrich IP, domain, URL, and hash evidence.
Threat intel suggests the identity may be the attacker pivot rather than only the victim.
Identity risk is high enough to inspect endpoint process ancestry and network sessions.
Endpoint artifacts need SIEM and cloud timeline correlation before containment.
Map-reduce logs expose a second-stage IOC, so Log Analysis routes back to Enrichment.
New enrichment updates the user/session blast-radius hypothesis.
Updated identity evidence requires a second endpoint check on the affected host.
Endpoint confirmation sends the graph back into log reduction for a final confidence score.
Second-pass timeline has enough confidence for actor/TTP matching.
Threat Intel returns the case to Supervisor for the final containment decision.
Supervisor chooses HITL containment after cyclic review raises confidence above threshold.

message.content: {"executiveSummary": "A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.", "rootCause": "The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.", "mitreMapping": ["T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage"], "timeline": ["2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)", "2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected", "2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking", "2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials", "2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18"], "agentRouting": ["Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.", "Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.", "Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.", "Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.", "Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.", "Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.", "Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.", "Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.", "Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.", "Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.", "Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.", "Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment."], "containmentActions": ["Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration", "Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions", "Reset MFA factors for the affected user"], "recommendations": ["Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations", "Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data", "Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints", "Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload"], "analystDecisions": ["Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity"], "toolResultSummary": ["VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.", "AbuseIPDB: IP"]}

parsedResponsePayload.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.
parsedResponsePayload.rootCause: The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.
mitreMapping: T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
timeline: 2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)
2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected
2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking
2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials
2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18
agentRouting: Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.
Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.
Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.
Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.
Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.
Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.
Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.
Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.
Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.
Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.
Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.
Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment.
containmentActions: Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration
Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions
Reset MFA factors for the affected user
recommendations: Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations
Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data
Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints
Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload
analystDecisions: Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity
toolResultSummary: VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.
AbuseIPDB: IP

message.content: {"executiveSummary": "A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.", "rootCause": "The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.", "mitreMapping": ["T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage"], "timeline": ["2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)", "2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected", "2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking", "2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials", "2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18"], "agentRouting": ["Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.", "Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.", "Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.", "Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.", "Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.", "Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.", "Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.", "Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.", "Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.", "Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.", "Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.", "Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment."], "containmentActions": ["Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration", "Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions", "Reset MFA factors for the affected user"], "recommendations": ["Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations", "Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data", "Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints", "Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload"], "analystDecisions": ["Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity"], "toolResultSummary": ["VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.", "AbuseIPDB: IP"]}

parsedResponsePayload.executiveSummary: A confirmed identity compromise and data exfiltration incident involved a compromised Finance user account, jsmith@ent.local, which was used from an unmanaged endpoint to upload confidential financial data to an actor-controlled cloud storage service.
parsedResponsePayload.rootCause: The compromise originated from an unmanaged endpoint, WS-FIN-042, where the user jsmith@ent.local executed a malicious payload leading to unauthorized access and data exfiltration.
mitreMapping: T1567.002 - Exfil Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
timeline: 2024-10-27T13:55:04Z - User jsmith@ent.local logs in via Okta from 10.5.2.14 (New York, US)
2024-10-27T14:18:05Z - Active Directory logon from unregistered endpoint WS-FIN-042 detected
2024-10-27T14:18:12Z - Okta session from 91.243.44.18 (Minsk, BY) indicates impossible travel or session hijacking
2024-10-27T14:19:45Z - Attacker accesses AWS Console using compromised credentials
2024-10-27T14:22:31Z - Successful PutObject action uploads confidential/Q3.xlsx to attacker IP 91.243.44.18
agentRouting: Supervisor initially routes alert cluster to Triage.
Triage forwards to Enrichment to expand context on IOCs.
Enrichment passes findings to Identity to validate user blast-radius.
Identity routes to Endpoint to inspect WS-FIN-042.
Endpoint forwards artifacts to Log Analysis to reconstruct timeline.
Log Analysis BACKTRACKS to Enrichment after discovering second-stage IOC.
Enrichment forwards updated context back to Identity.
Identity routes to Endpoint for a second host inspection.
Endpoint forwards confirmation to Log Analysis for final reduction.
Log Analysis forwards to Threat Intel for actor TTP mapping.
Threat Intel BACKTRACKS to Supervisor for final decision.
Supervisor INTERRUPTS for human approval before containment.
containmentActions: Isolate compromised host WS-FIN-042 to prevent further lateral movement or exfiltration
Disable or suspend the compromised user account jsmith@ent.local and revoke active sessions
Reset MFA factors for the affected user
recommendations: Enforce strict conditional access policies blocking unmanaged devices and impossible travel locations
Review and restrict AWS S3 bucket policies to ensure least privilege access for financial data
Implement endpoint detection and response (EDR) on allFinance department endpoints
Conduct a company-wide phishing awareness training and scan for the initial malware payload
analystDecisions: Approve host isolation for WS-FIN-042 for 45 minutes based on high exfiltration risk and malicious C2 network activity
toolResultSummary: VirusTotal: IP 91.243.44.18 confirmed as malicious with high abuse confidence, associated with C2 and phishing.
AbuseIPDB: IP
